segments.text: Cell signaling or cell-cell communication

The version in Chapter 37 – ack !
Don’t learn this.

water-soluble hormones
receptor protein in cell membrane
activates cytoplasmic protein
signal transduction cascade
cell response
shape change
metabolism change
change gene expression
Same hormone – different responses from different cell types

Same hormone – different responses from different cell types
same receptors
Same hormone – different responses from different cell types

Plant Hormones
Gibberellin/ Gibberellic Acid
seed germination
breaking winter dormancy
fruit growth

Plant Hormones
Gibberellin/ Gibberellic Acid
seed germination
breaking winter dormancy
fruit growth
Abscisic acid (ABA)
inhibits seed germination
promotes bud dormancy
accumulation of storage molecules
stress hormone – produced by:
dormant buds
wilted leaves
regulates guard cells  closes stomata (reduces water stress/wilting)
promotes leaf abscision

seed dormancy
seed germination
Don’t learn structures!

Gravitropism
roots grow down
shoots grow up

Gravity sensor
in root tips and shoot tips

Auxin ( Indole Acetic Acid or IAA)
Don’t learn structure!

auxin is produced in shoot and root tips
transported up or down

auxin accumulates
on “lower” side
Auxin inhibits cell elongation in roots

Phototropism
shoots grow toward light
(will over-ride gravitropism )
Shoots respond specifically to blue light
Blue light is best for photosynthesis
Light-sensing cells are in shoot tip

Permeable barrier (agar):
signal diffuses down stem
from tip, causes bending
Impermeable barrier:
signal can’t diffuse down
stem, shoot doesn’t bend

Auxin promotes cell
elongation in shoots
shoot bends toward
light

Gravitropism
Auxin accumulates on lower side
promotes cell elongation
shoot grows up, against gravity
Phototropism
Auxin accumulates on shaded side
promotes cell elongation
shoot grows toward light
Auxin in shoot system

auxin is produced in shoot and root tips
transported up or down

Apical dominance
Auxin transported down from
shoot apical meristem
inhibits lateral bud outgrowth
Remove shoot apical meristem – lateral buds form new shoots
pruning or “pinching back” apical meristems promotes lateral bud outgrowth

As shoot apex grows up – source of auxin is farther away
less auxin = less lateral bud inhibition
lateral bud outgrowth promoted by cytokinin
[ cytokinin ] > [ auxin ] leads to lateral bud outgrowth

Cytokinins
promote cell division (“cytokinesis”)
produced in roots
transported up plant by xylem
lateral bud outgrowth
cell division in apical meristems
flower and fruit formation
cytokinins act in opposition to auxin
these two regulate the overall shape of the plant along apical-basal axis

Ethylene
a gas – influences neighbors, not just producing plant
promotes fruit ripening
tissue senescence
fruit and leaf abscission
